reasoning: Uses node:20-alpine which defaults to root. No USER instruction present and no entrypoint that drops privileges. Risk is mitigated by: (a) validation-only infrastructure, never deployed to production; (b) only activated via the 'db-migration' Docker Compose profile (opt-in). However, the container runs as root with a writable /workspace/out volume mount shared across the Compose stack, and has a host port binding (5001). Low severity because of the profile gating and local-only context, but the root-process state is real and unmitigated.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. The node:20-alpine image ships a pre-created non-root 'node' user. The WORKDIR /app is owned by root so also add 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' before switching users, or use '--chown=node:node' on COPY instructions.

Mounts /workspace/out as a writable shared volume. Host port 3001 is bound. Low severity due to profile gating and local-only context, but the root-process state is real and unmitigated.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. Use '--chown=node:node' on COPY instructions or 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' before switching users.

reasoning: Uses node:20-alpine which defaults to root. No USER instruction present and no entrypoint that drops privileges. This container starts by default (no Compose profile gate) whenever 'docker compose up' is run. Host port 4000 is bound and reachable from localhost. Mounts /workspace/out as a writable shared volume. The container also has a /__admin/* control API used by scenario-runner to inject fault modes. Running as root means a process compromise has broader filesystem and network reach within the container. Still validation-only infrastructure but higher exposure than profile-gated services.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. Ensure /app is owned by node before switching: add 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' or use '--chown=node:node' on the COPY instructions.

reasoning: Uses node:20-alpine which defaults to root. No USER instruction present and no entrypoint that drops privileges. This is the primary application container under test — always started (no profile gate), host port 3000 bound, PostgreSQL DATABASE_URL in environment, OTel exporter configured, and a shared writable /workspace/out volume. It is the most realistic app container in the validation stack and the one most likely to receive simulated external traffic from loadgen. Running as root is unmitigated. Still local/validation-only, but this is the highest-exposure container in the stack.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. Use '--chown=node:node' on all COPY instructions (COPY --chown=node:node ... ) or add 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' immediately before 'USER node'.

reasoning: Uses node:20-alpine which defaults to root. No USER instruction present and no entrypoint that drops privileges. Loadgen starts by default (no profile gate), host port 8080 is bound, and it exposes a /__admin/profile control API used to change load patterns at runtime. The admin API has no authentication. Running as root amplifies the impact of any code execution via the admin API. Validation-only infrastructure, but the unauthenticated control surface and default-on status make this a higher exposure than profile-gated containers.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. Ensure /app ownership: add 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' or use '--chown=node:node' on COPY instructions.

reasoning: Uses node:20-alpine which defaults to root. No USER instruction present and no entrypoint that drops privileges. However, this container has no host port binding — it is purely an internal orchestrator that runs a single scenario script and exits. It mounts /workspace/scenarios read-only and /workspace/out writable. Running as root means it can write arbitrary data to the shared out/ volume, but attack surface is minimal since there is no network interface exposed to the host. Lowest severity in the set due to the absence of any inbound network exposure.
fix: Add 'USER node' before the CMD instruction. Use '--chown=node:node' on COPY instructions or add 'RUN chown -R node:node /app' before switching users.
